audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for hour/glory prayer, age-long life as knowing Father/Son, keeping the name, protection, sanctification in truth, mission, oneness language, glory/love indwelling.

verses.restored: Jesus spoke these things, and lifting his eyes to heaven, said, Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son, so that the Son may glorify you.
Just as you gave him authority over all flesh/embodied humanity, so that all whom you have given him, he may give them age-long life.
This is age-long life: that they may know you, the only true Divine, and Jesus Messiah whom you sent.
I glorified you on the earth, completing the work that you have given me to do.
And now, Father, glorify me beside yourself with the glory I had beside you before the world was.
I revealed your name to the humans whom you gave me from the world. They were yours, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
Now they have known that all things you have given me are from you.
For the words that you gave me I have given them, and they received them and knew truly that I came out from you, and they trusted that you sent me.
I ask concerning them; I do not ask concerning the world, but concerning those whom you have given me, because they are yours.
All my things are yours, and yours mine, and I have been glorified in them.
I am no longer in the world, and they are in the world, and I come to you. Holy Father, keep them in your name that you have given me, so that they may be one just as we are.
When I was with them, I kept them in your name that you have given me. I guarded them, and none of them was lost except the son of loss, so that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
But now I come to you, and these things I speak in the world, so that they may have my joy fulfilled in themselves.
I have given them your word, and the world hated them because they are not from the world, just as I am not from the world.
I do not ask that you take them out of the world, but that you keep them from the harmful one.
They are not from the world, just as I am not from the world.
Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth.
Just as you sent me into the world, I also sent them into the world.
For them I sanctify myself, so that they also may be sanctified in truth.
I do not ask concerning these only, but also concerning those trusting into me through their word,
so that all may be one, just as you, Father, are in me and I in you, that they also may be in us, so that the world may trust that you sent me.
The glory that you have given me I have given them, so that they may be one just as we are one,
I in them and you in me, so that they may be completed into one, so that the world may know that you sent me and loved them just as you loved me.
Father, what you have given me, I will that where I am, those also may be with me, so that they may behold my glory that you have given me, because you loved me before the foundation of the world.
Righteous Father, the world did not know you, but I knew you, and these knew that you sent me.
I made your name known to them and will make it known, so that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them.
